question: Explain the advantages of modular design in NPM.

nuscale_ch01_0391.title: SRP 9.5.1.2, Rev 0: Risk-Informed, Performance-Based Fire Protection ProgramAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0391.summary: The section explains that the development and implementation of a risk-informed, performance-based fire protection program (SRP 9.5.1.2, Rev 0) is not applicable to the NuScale design certification applicant, but is instead the responsibility of Combined License (COL) applicants who reference the NuScale design and choose to implement 10 CFR 50.48(c). NuScale’s application addresses relevant guidance only to the extent necessary to ensure COL applicants can meet these requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0004.title: Containment Type
nuscale_ch01_0004.summary: The NuScale containment vessel is a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and operates under vacuum during normal conditions, eliminating the need for insulation. The NuScale reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor (SMR) with natural circulation and no external piping for steam generators or pressurizer, and each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant yielding approximately 570 MWe net output.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios and reduce reliance on external power, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to traditional light water reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0390.title: SRP 9.5.1.1, Rev 0: Fire Protection ProgramII.7 Operational Program and Proposed Implementation Milestones
nuscale_ch01_0390.summary: This section explains that the operational fire protection program and its implementation milestones are not applicable to the NuScale design certification application, as these are the responsibility of the Combined License (COL) applicant. NuScale’s application partially conforms to certain fire protection criteria but defers operational program development and risk-informed fire protection measures to the COL applicant referencing the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0022.title: Engineered Safety Feature Materials
nuscale_ch01_0022.summary: The NuScale reactor’s containment vessel (CNV) is kept under vacuum during normal operation, which reduces moisture and corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits oxygen for safety. Its passive safety features—including elimination of large external coolant loops and reliance on passive cooling—result in improved safety, lower core damage frequency, and a smaller radioactive source term compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, the use of representative conceptual design information (CDI), and notes that each COL applicant must reference the NuScale FSAR while providing site-specific details as required by regulations.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information alongside written descriptions. It references tables and figures that list and explain the symbols and diagrams used, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0021.title: Engineered Safety Features and Emergency Systems
nuscale_ch01_0021.summary: The NuScale reactor’s containment vessel (CNV) is kept under vacuum during normal operation, which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems—including decay heat removal and emergency core cooling—operate without external power, eliminating large-break LOCA risks and resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0008.title: Format and Content
nuscale_ch01_0008.summary: The section describes the NuScale containment vessel as a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events, with internal vacuum pressure during normal operation. The NuScale reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor (SMR) that uses natural circulation and eliminates the need for external piping and coolant pumps. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) produces 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), with a 12-module plant generating a net output of approximately 570 MWe, and applicants referencing the design must provide construction schedules.
